headline: Honda downgraded to Equal Weight from Overweight at Morgan Stanley
summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Hiroto Segawa downgraded Honda (HMC) to Equal Weight from Overweight with a price target of 1,600 yen, down from 2,000 yen. The firm recommends “selective stock-picking” in Japan’s auto and mobility group, saying industry headwinds are picking up amid rising raw material costs and emerging geopolitical risk. headline: Morgan Stanley Caps Private Credit Fund Redemptions; Stock Falls
summary: A Morgan Stanley private credit fund is among the latest to say it won’t give back all the money to investors wanting out.  The bank’s North Haven private-income fund told investors Wednesday it received quarterly requests making up around 10.9% of the fund’s $7.6 billion in assets, but would stick to a 5% limit.  In its letter, Morgan Stanley said the fund wasn’t designed to offer full liquidity because of the nature of its investments, and that credit fundamentals across the underlying portfolio have been broadly stable.

raw.headline: Morgan Stanley Caps Private Credit Fund Redemptions; Stock Falls

The bank’s North Haven private-income fund told investors Wednesday it received quarterly requests making up around 10.9% of the fund’s $7.6 billion in assets, but would stick to a 5% limit.  In its letter, Morgan Stanley said the fund wasn’t designed to offer full liquidity because of the nature of its investments, and that credit fundamentals across the underlying portfolio have been broadly stable.

headline: Morgan Stanley Private Credit Fund Hit With Redemption Requests
summary: A Morgan Stanley private credit fund is among the latest to say it won’t give back all the money to investors wanting out.  The bank’s North Haven private-income fund in a letter to investors Wednesday said it received quarterly requests making up around 10.9% of the fund’s $7.6 billion in assets, but would stick to a 5% limit.  In the letter, Morgan Stanley said the fund wasn’t designed to offer full liquidity because of the nature of its investments, and that credit fundamentals across the underlying portfolio have been broadly stable.

In the letter, Morgan Stanley said the fund wasn’t designed to offer full liquidity because of the nature of its investments, and that credit fundamentals across the underlying portfolio have been broadly stable.
